post.summary: Change the status of a campaign
post.description:
Change the status of a campaign to a desired status.

The status can be one of the following: active, inactive.

Status transition is accompanied by side effects, e.g., automation execution.

get.description: Get the status of a campaign's automation job
get.summary: Get the status of a campaign's automation job
Get portals usage info for a campaign
get.description: Get the list of portals and its widgets where the campaign is used.

post.description:
Retrigger automation executions for specific campaign recipients that have failed.

This endpoint starts new automation executions for the specified recipients
using the campaign's associated automation flow. Only recipients with
automation_status 'failed' will be processed. Recipients with other statuses
(success, pending, in_progress, cancelled) will be skipped to prevent
accidentally retriggering successful or ongoing automations.

post.summary: Set up a campaign with related entities and configurations
post.description:
Creates a `campaign` entity together with its related entities and configurations in a single call.
Used by the campaign wizard UI, but not restricted to it.

Match campaigns based on target entities.

This endpoint returns the list of campaigns where the provided entities are part of the target.
Find targets from the provided list that include the provide entities.

Transform target filters into Lucene queries for the provided target IDs.
Returns the transformed query string for each target along with any errors encountered.

post.summary: Create a recipient associated with a campaign
post.description: Creates a new recipient associated with a campaign.

patch.summary: Update portal status for a campaign recipient
patch.description:
Updates the portal status for a specific campaign recipient.
The portal_status_updated_at timestamp is automatically set when the status changes.

Status transition rules:
- From 'sent': can change to 'seen', 'dismissed', or 'clicked'
- From 'seen': can change to 'dismissed' or 'clicked'
- From 'dismissed' or 'clicked': cannot be changed (final states)

get.description: Get a paginated list of recipients for a campaign.

parameters.name: automation_status
parameters.description:
Filter by automation status. Repeat the parameter to match multiple
statuses in one request
(e.g. automation_status=failed&automation_status=cancelled).

oneOf.title: MessageError
oneOf.description: An error object containing a human-readable message.

oneOf.title: CodeError
oneOf.description: An error object containing a specific machine-readable error code.

code.description: A specific machine-readable error code indicating a known error condition.

oneOf.title: StatusedError
oneOf.description: An error object containing a message and an explicit status, typically from a more generic error handler.

error.example: An unexpected error occurred during processing.

status.description: An explicit status code in the body, often mirroring the HTTP status but provided for programmatic access.

ClientError.description:
Describes the structure of a client error response, which can be one of several types:
1. `MessageError`: Contains a 'message' field for general descriptive errors.
2. `CodeError`: Contains a 'code' field for specific, machine-readable error codes.
3. `StatusedError`: Contains 'error' and 'status' fields.
The HTTP status code of the response itself (e.g., 400, 404, 409) will always indicate the overall error category.

BaseEntityOwner.description:
The user / organization owning this entity.

Note: Owner implicitly has access to the entity regardless of ACLs.

BaseEntityAcl.description: Access control list (ACL) for an entity. Defines sharing access to external orgs or users.

_org.description: Organization Id the entity belongs to

status.description: The status of the automation job

entity_refs.description: List of entities (e.g. Contacts or Contracts) that should be part of the campaign target.

campaign_ids.description: List of campaign IDs to check.

entity_refs.description: List of entities (e.g. Contacts or Contracts) that should be part of the targets.

target_ids.description: List of target IDs to check.
List of target IDs to transform into queries.

query.description: Transformed query string, or null if transformation failed.

error.description: Error message if query transformation failed.

recipient_ids.description: List of recipient IDs to retrigger automations for

result.description: The result of the retriggering operation

execution_id.description: The ID of the new automation execution (if successful)

error.description: The error message if the operation failed

SetupCampaignRequest.description:
Discriminated by `type`. Each campaign variant has its own request shape;
new variants are added by introducing a new schema and extending the `oneOf` list.

name.description: Optional name of the product recommendation. Defaults to the campaign name.

source_product_id.description: Optional source product entity ID for the recommendation.

source_price_id.description: Optional source price entity ID for the recommendation.

offers.description: Offer blocks for the product_recommendation entity.

highlight_config.description: Opaque per-item highlight/comparison config persisted as-is on the entity.

target_ids.description:
List of target entity IDs to attach to the campaign. Today only a single
entry is supported (campaign.target is has_one) but the array shape is kept
for forward-compatibility — only `target_ids[0]` is used.

journey.description:
Optional journey configuration selected in the wizard. Only sent by the
consumer once a journey has been chosen.

channels.description:
Optional delivery channel configuration. Each channel is only sent by the
consumer once it has been enabled and fully configured in the wizard.

portal_widget.description:
Portal widget placement for the product recommendation block. Only sent by
the consumer once both a portal and a block have been selected.

email.description:
Email campaign delivery. An automation flow (legacy) and/or an email
template can be provided to send the campaign emails. The selection is
persisted as the campaign's `flow_id`. If `automation_id` is provided it is
used as-is; otherwise a flow is created from `template_id`. Only sent by the
consumer once one has been selected.

EpilotAuth.description: Authorization header with epilot OAuth2 bearer token

RecipientIdPathParam.name: recipient_id
RecipientIdPathParam.description: The entity ID of the recipient

ClientErrorResponse.description: Any error based on client data errors

ServerErrorResponse.description: Any error based on the server-side

next.description: Cursor for next page of results

MatchCampaignsResponse.description: List of campaigns where the target entities match the given entities.

MatchTargetsResponse.description: List of targets where the given entities are found.

CampaignPortalsResponse.description: List of portals and its widgets where the campaign is used

SetupCampaignResponse.description:
Discriminated by `type`, mirroring the request. Each campaign variant has its
own response shape; new variants extend the `oneOf` list alongside the request.

RetriggerAutomationsResponse.description: Response to a request to retrigger automations for campaign recipients

message.description: A confirmation message indicating the request was received and processed
